programmes.show_description: If You Wait By The River Long Enough: Ah Sahm, Hong and Young Jun are held to account for their secret mission. A shocking scene rocks the Blake household. (S2, ep 7)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Wales: From violent drunks and drug dealers to a dangerous domestic incident, this week's fix focuses on cops based in West Wales. (S1, ep 4)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S11, ep 5)

CBP officers question a Mexican national with odd travel plans and in Port Huron, a traveller's past may come back to haunt him. (S1, ep 18)
An officer suspects a US resident returning from the Caribbean may be hiding something and two construction workers wind up at the border by mistake. (S1, ep 19)

A frequent visitor of drug source countries is questioned in New York and a Canadian cowboy may be a mule. (S1, ep 9)
A bus passenger presents curious concoctions at JFK Airport and a Chicago canine sniffs out a stash of cash. (S1, ep 10)

Invasive Procedures: The crew must fight for Jadzia's life when a desperate Trill takes the group hostage and steals the Dax symbiont.
Small Victories: SG-1 must face and destroy the replicators that have made their way to Earth. (S4, ep 1)
Repentance: The crew members re-evaluate their ethical beliefs, as well as those of an alien criminal justice system, when they rescue a Nygean prison warden and his prisoners. (S7, ep 13)
Where No One Has Gone Before: The Enterprise travel into unknown territory more than a billion light years away when a test on the ship's propulsion system backfires.
Herd Logic: A herd of rhinoceros-like creatures come through an anomaly. The team arrive at the scene, except for Danny who is busy infiltrating Johnson's HQ. (S3, ep 9)
The Breaking Point: Having held off German attempts to overrun Bastogne, Easy Company now faces the task of taking the nearby town of Foy from the enemy. Contains flashing images. (7 of 10)
The Patrol: With the war winding down, Easy Co moves into an Alsatian town near the German border where they sleep just across the river from the Nazis. Contains flashing images. (8 of 10)
Zerstorer Shrugged: Nick goes back to his routes in search of answers as the Zerstorer arrives in Portland with a trail of bodies in its wake. (S6, ep 12)
All Enemies, Foreign and Domestic: The police shut down Chinatown as they hunt for a killer. Meanwhile, the Hop Wei make a major move. (S2, ep 8)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Devon/Cornwall: Adrenaline-fuelled documentary following Britain's police. This week's fix sees them tackling robbers, prostitutes and speed merchants in the West Country. (S1, ep 6)

At Detroit Metro Airport, a pregnant Chinese national has CBP asking questions and a security breach on the tarmac leads officers to a jet bound for Haiti. (S1, ep 20)
A Canadian is interviewed under oath after CBP discovers pictures of pot plants on his phone. Without paperwork, it could be curtains for a Venezuelan conductor. (S1, ep 21)

In Chicago, officers find some questionable medicine, hikers get into trouble and an architect's story seems suspicious. (S1, ep 11)
Outbound officers search for a fugitive in Washington State and a Vegas-bound Dutch cook is grilled in secondary at Detroit Metro Airport. (S1, ep 12)

Cardassian: A young Cardassian, orphaned in the war and raised by Bajorans, causes turmoil on the station when his people attempt to reclaim him.
The Other Side: SG-1 responds to a plea for help from a civilisation that has traced its roots back to Earth. (S4, ep 2)
Prophecy: Voyager encounters a group of nomadic Klingons aboard a generational ship, who are convinced that Tom and B'Elanna's unborn child is their saviour. (S7, ep 14)
The Last Outpost: The away teams of the USS Enterprise and Ferengi starships face an inquisition by a mysterious life form as they are held captive at an unknown planet.
The Chase Continues: Danny, Connor and Abby must pursue Helen Cutter through a series of anomalies in order to stop her from wiping out humanity's ancestors. Season finale. (S3, ep 10)
Why We Fight: Easy Company find an abandoned concentration camp. While helping the survivors and burying the dead, news comes of Hitler's suicide. (9 of 10)
Points: The soldiers celebrate the end of the war, but their joy is short-lived as they learn not everyone has enough service points to be sent home. Contains flashing images. (10 of 10)
The End: Nick faces off against a seemingly unstoppable enemy in this final episode of the fantasy drama. (S6, ep 13)
